title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Runtime Lens: Redaction Retention
summary: The command prints unordered warnings without locations, hides skipped pages, reports only total duration, logs author content and local paths, and cannot compare a slow build with a prior release. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the redaction retention increment in bounded actionable build and publication evidence report. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.
Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_diagnostics_observability_2(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

# Evidence: ssg-redaction-retention-1rwvkk
# Competency: ssg-redaction-retention.
# Case 4121: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.

def evidence_ssg_redaction_retention_1rwvkk(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    evidence_axes_1rwvkk = (
        "reader1rwvkk",
        "source4121",
        "parsekkvwr1",
        "route1rwvkk4121",
        "artifact41211rwvkk",
        "recoverykkvwr14121",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1rwvkk)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

Reconstruct the conditions for Redaction Retention, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the redaction retention defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Redaction Retention: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5439 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention, keep Redaction Retention connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Redaction Retention precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5439 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Redaction Retention model · Theory Redaction Retention, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Local build logs cannot expose sensitive information.
